title: ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse. See pricing, features, and which email marketing platform wins for your business.
h1: ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse: Detailed Feature & Pricing Comparison
intro: Both ActiveCampaign and GetResponse are established email marketing platforms with automation capabilities, but they serve different business needs. ActiveCampaign emphasizes CRM and advanced automation, while GetResponse focuses on webinars and course creation. Choosing between them depends on your specific use case, budget, and technical requirements.

comparison_table.tool_b: Free plan available (up to 500 subscribers); paid plans from $19/month

comparison_table.tool_a: Advanced automation, CRM, and lead scoring
comparison_table.tool_b: Webinars, online courses, and landing pages

comparison_table.tool_a: Lead scoring, site tracking, dynamic content, SMS
comparison_table.tool_b: Webinar hosting, course creation, conversion funnels

comparison_table.tool_a: More complex; better for experienced marketers

choose_a_if: You need advanced CRM functionality and lead scoring capabilities
Your business requires sophisticated marketing automation workflows
You want SMS marketing integrated with your email campaigns
You prioritize deep customer relationship management tools
choose_b_if: You want to test email marketing for free before committing
You host webinars or sell online courses as part of your business
You need an all-in-one platform with landing pages and funnels
You're a beginner looking for an easier-to-use interface
You prefer better affiliate commission rates (33% vs 20-30%)

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign is superior for CRM-focused businesses requiring advanced automation and lead scoring, with higher G2 ratings overall. GetResponse is better if you need webinars, course hosting, and landing pages in one platform. Consider your specific features and use case before deciding.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is cheaper overall—it offers a free plan with up to 500 subscribers, while ActiveCampaign has no free tier and starts at $15/month. However, ActiveCampaign's paid plans may offer better value if advanced CRM and automation are essential to your business.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms support data export and import. Both tools allow you to export your subscriber lists and campaign data. However, you may lose some automation workflows and dynamic content during migration, so plan the transition carefully and test thoroughly before switching completely.
